match_info.result: Delhi Capitals won by 6 wickets (with 11 balls remaining)

volatility_index.interpretation: One-sided chase — DC controlled after Rizvi arrived

turning_points.label: The Double Strike in Over 3 — Mukesh Kumar's Powerplay Blitz
turning_points.description: With MI reeling at 18/0 after two overs, Mukesh Kumar struck twice in over 3 to wreck the top order. Rickelton (9) and Tilak Varma (0) both fell to catches, leaving MI at 18/2. The collapse neutralised the fearsome MI batting line-up before it could get going.
turning_points.wp_impact: MI's projected total dropped from 175+ to sub-160 territory

turning_points.label: Nissanka Departs at 73/3 — The Match Hangs
turning_points.description: At 73/3 with DC still needing 90 from 60 balls, Pathum Nissanka skied a Santner drag-down to Markande at short fine leg. In strode Sameer Rizvi — DC's impact sub — to join David Miller. At that moment, DC's win probability was precariously balanced near 50%.
turning_points.wp_impact: Win probability dipped to ~45% for DC at the fall of Nissanka

turning_points.label: The Rizvi Explosion — Overs 10-16
turning_points.description: From the moment Sameer Rizvi arrived, DC's required rate was 9.0. What followed was a sustained assault: Rizvi took DC from 73/3 to 151/3, scoring 78 of those 78 runs in 6.1 overs. Shardul Thakur bore the brunt — 0-41 in 3 overs. The match effectively ended before over 17.
turning_points.wp_impact: Win probability surged from 48% to 82% during Rizvi's assault

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If Shardul Thakur Had Bowled at His Average?
counterfactual_scenarios.original_outcome: Delhi Capitals won by 6 wickets with 11 balls remaining
counterfactual_scenarios.lola_outcome: Delhi Capitals would still win but need ~148 off 20 overs — making it far tighter
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative: Shardul Thakur bled 41 runs from his 3 overs — an economy of 13.66 in a chase where DC needed 163. Had he matched his IPL career rate of 8.5 runs per over, DC would have needed 148 instead of 164 — a target 16 runs higher going into the death. With MI's batting already at 162, a target of ~179 would have shifted the required rate for Rizvi's partnership from 9.0 to 10.8, requiring a different risk profile entirely.

counterfactual_scenarios.title: What If Rickelton Had Converted That Start?
counterfactual_scenarios.change: Ryan Rickelton scores 40 instead of 9 — lasts until over 8 instead of falling in over 3
counterfactual_scenarios.original_outcome: MI set 162. DC won by 6 wickets with 11 balls remaining.
counterfactual_scenarios.lola_outcome: MI set ~193. DC need 194. Required rate for Rizvi's partnership becomes 12.2 — a different chase.
counterfactual_scenarios.narrative: Rickelton's dismissal in over 3 for just 9 off 11 balls was the first domino. In MI's batting order, Rickelton had shown form this season — there was every reason to expect a substantial innings. Had he converted to 40 (his current-form benchmark), he and Rohit could have built a 100-run stand before the 10th over. MI's total climbs to 193. A target of 194 turns DC's chase from comfortable into a knife-edge proposition — even Rizvi's extraordinary 51-ball 90 would have delivered a much tighter finish.

commentary_gems.text: Bouncer is pulled straight to deep midwicket. It's been bouncers or yorkers this over, this was a slow one that was straight. Dhir waited on it and pulled from shoulder-height. Didn't lose his shape, just didn't connect well enough

Umpire takes his time and gives it out. Given out lbw, SKY reviews. He was square-on. Back foot in the crease, front just on it. So, height may not be an issue even though he's hit on the right thigh. Was a back-of-length ball that landed just outside off, SKY went for the pull. Got beaten by the pace and lack of bounce. Impact - off stump, once that came up, SKY walked as he knew it'd be hitting the wickets. 122ks but it didn't look like a slower one on first view.

Very close to the ropes, but Mukesh takes it!He was running to the right from deep square leg, took a high catch, held on as he decelerated and hugged Kuldeep running in from the other side. Vipraj pitched up the topspinner and Rutherford went for the slog sweep. Got a decent chunk too

Oh, has he got something on it? KL has been strangled down the leg side!Chahar fires this full and going down leg. Rahul tries to glance it fine and gets a thin inside edge on it. Rickelton pounces on it, diving to his left. Horror dismissal for him!

Oh, he's skied that and Markande runs a long way to grab it! This is a huge moment in the game. This is dragged down a little and Nissanka looks to swivel and pull but ends up with a top edge that balloons up behind him. Markande runs across from short fine leg and grabs it. Big, big blow for DC

Bumrah is confident with the lbw appeal, not given. Ball goes to the keeper, Rizvi had to run back again as the throw came through. No direct hit, would've been out had Rickelton hit. No review taken. Replay shows the low full toss took the inside edge as Rizvi looked to flick through midwicket

game_changers.name: Sameer Rizvi
game_changers.reason: Rizvi, DC's impact sub, turned a nervy chase into a one-sided exercise in authority. Joining at 73/3 with DC needing 90 from 60 balls, he dismantled Shardul Thakur and Corbin Bosch to score 78 of his 90 off just 45 balls after his arrival. His partnership of 78 with Miller in 43 balls made the match safe before over 17.

game_changers.name: Mukesh Kumar
game_changers.key_stat: 3-0-26-2, destroyed MI top order in overs 2-3
game_changers.reason: Mukesh Kumar's dismissals of Rickelton (9) and Tilak Varma (0) off consecutive balls in the third over effectively ended MI's innings as a contest. MI at 18/2 in 2.5 overs never recovered the platform that their batting depth needed.

game_changers.name: Suryakumar Yadav
game_changers.reason: SKY's 51 off 36 balls was the only innings that genuinely challenged DC's attack, and his partnership with Rohit — 53 runs in 41 balls — was MI's only sustained attacking phase. His dismissal to Ngidi's lbw in over 16 at 122/5 immediately made the target look below-par.

game_changers.name: Jasprit Bumrah
game_changers.reason: Despite going wicketless, Bumrah conceded just 21 off 4 overs (5.25 economy, 13 dots) and was DC's one phase of sustained pressure. The problem: DC scored so freely off the other bowlers — Thakur went for 41 and Bosch for 39 — that Bumrah's excellence was offset. A study in how one elite bowler cannot win a T20 alone.
